entries.title: Design an LLM inference serving platform at scale

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: KV cache memory management (the actual bottleneck)
Deep dive 2: scheduling and fairness under load
Deep dive 3: cost and capacity planning

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: access control ordering (the detail that separates Staff+ from Senior)

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: the gateway pattern for side-effecting actions

entries.title: Design a feature store / fine-tuning data pipeline

sections.heading: Deep dive 2: data lineage and reproducibility
Deep dive 3: freshness vs. compute cost trade-off
Deep dive 4: exact reproducibility as a bit-level contract, not a policy statement
Deep dive 5: schema evolution, dual-write sync, and RTBF (interview-critical, keep short)

entries.title: Design a content moderation and safety system for generated content

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: real, working input/output guardrails, not a placeholder
Deep dive 2: routing risk signals to the right response, not one binary gate
Deep dive 3: the human review loop as a real system, not an afterthought

entries.title: Design a multimodal search / recommendation system

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: ANN index choice at billion-item scale — the actual serving-time bottleneck
Deep dive 2: multiple candidate-generation sources, not one
Deep dive 3: multimodal representation — fusion vs. separate signals

Deep dive 4: serving degradation and deletion blast radius

entries.title: Design an LLM evaluation and observability platform

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: fixtures that validate vs. fixtures that actually gate
Deep dive 2: trace-linked evals — connecting production behavior back to eval scores
Deep dive 3: cost as an evaluation dimension, not a separate concern
Deep dive 4: trace privacy and volume (interview-critical)

entries.title: Design a fine-tuning / RLHF training pipeline at scale

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: reward model quality is its own first-class problem
Deep dive 2: cost and fault tolerance at multi-node scale
Deep dive 3: the eval gate before promotion — and what it must check beyond "is it better"

entries.title: Design a multi-tenant AI platform

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: shared vs. dedicated capacity, and the real mitigation for noisy neighbors
Deep dive 2: customization without fragmenting the platform
Deep dive 3: the AI-specific isolation problem generic multi-tenancy design doesn't cover
Deep dive 4: quota-ledger failure mode (must say out loud)

entries.title: Design a sandboxing architecture for AI agent code execution

sections.heading: Deep dive 2: prompt injection as a first-class threat model

entries.title: Design an on-device / edge AI inference architecture

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: on-device vs. cloud-first, and why the trade-off differs from classic edge computing

Deep dive 3: what a "no-retention" cloud tier actually requires architecturally

entries.title: Design a training-data provenance and IP-risk architecture

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: provenance tracking through transformation, not just at ingestion
Deep dive 2: can you prove a model didn't retain a specific removed source?
Deep dive 3: synthetic data and the model-collapse risk

entries.title: Design durable execution for long-running AI agents

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: durable execution engines — the real, named pattern this maps to

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: conversation context vs GPU memory

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: why training and serving region strategy are not the same problem

entries.title: Design disaster recovery for a model-serving platform

sections.heading: Where this actually gets asked — and a fabricated source caught and rejected
Deep dive 1: RTO/RPO tiering by workload, since one number doesn't fit both training and serving
Deep dive 2: rollback as the actual first line of defense, not regional failover
Deep dive 3: what "recovery" means when the asset is mostly stateless
Deep dive 4: warm standby vs cold RTO reality

entries.title: Design the network architecture for distributed training

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: RDMA/RoCE and InfiniBand vs. standard Ethernet
Deep dive 2: topology-aware scheduling as the software half of this problem
Deep dive 3: the straggler problem — a single slow link degrades the whole job
Deep dive 4: the control-plane network this contrast is against, made real

entries.title: Design the security and compliance architecture for an AI system

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: model weights as a uniquely high-value asset — the AI-specific wrinkle
Deep dive 2: secrets management — where this org found a real bug, not a hypothetical
Deep dive 3: compliance certifications as market-access gates, not engineering choices
Deep dive 4: inference-time boundary and data lineage

entries.title: Design container orchestration and cost optimization for AI workloads

sections.heading: Deep dive 1: managed orchestration vs. Kubernetes, with real numbers from actually deploying both
Deep dive 2: real bugs only a real deployment surfaced — not a Terraform-plan exercise
Deep dive 3: bin-packing and idle-GPU cost as the AI-specific optimization
Deep dive 4: preemption tiers and GPU node loss
